message:
fix(raptorq): GF256 c=1 addmul fast path and SIMD threshold fix

Three fixes in the GF256 SIMD layer:

1. gf256_addmul_slices2 c=1 fast path: when the coefficient is ONE,
   now routes through gf256_add_slices2() (fused dual-lane XOR) when
   the buffer sizes justify it, instead of always falling back to two
   independent add_slice calls. This enables SIMD acceleration for the
   c=1 case on qualifying buffer sizes.

2. gf256_add_slices2 SIMD threshold: the short-circuit condition for
   AVX2 and NEON was checking src_a.len() < 32 && src_b.len() < 32,
   meaning one short lane could force the entire operation into scalar
   mode even if the other lane was large enough for SIMD. Changed to
   src_a.len().min(src_b.len()) < threshold, so the fused path is
   used whenever the shorter lane meets the SIMD minimum.

3. Benchmark: new addmul_slices2_c1_auto and addmul_slices2_c1_sequential
   benchmark variants, plus bit-exactness validation for c=1 path.

New tests: addmul_slices2_with_one_matches_two_independent_add_slice_calls,
add_slices2_shorter_lane_below_simd_width_matches_two_independent_add_slice_calls.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
